name: From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar — circular route
name_original: De Santa Maria da Feira a Ovar — percurso circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Feira offer a diverse landscape for exploration. The region encompasses a mix of urban areas, agricultural lands, and natural spaces, extending towards the coast. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, from flatter sections suitable for leisurely rides to more undulating paths with moderate elevation gains. The area is characterized by its greenways, river valleys, and proximity to coastal towns like Ovar and Furadouro. Best touring cycling routes around Feira The most popular touring cycling route is From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar — circular route, a 15.2 miles (24.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This route connects the inland town of Santa Maria da Feira with the coastal town of Ovar. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Maceda Picnic Area  – Praia do Furadouro loop from Santa Maria da Feira, a moderate 19.7 miles (31.6 km) path. This route offers a blend of natural parkland and coastal scenery near the beach. Local touring cyclists also love the Castle of Santa Maria da Feira – Malaposta loop from São João de Ver, a 37.1 miles (59.7 km) trail leading through varied inland terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Feira is defined by its greenways, river valleys, and coastal stretches, offering a mix of natural and urban environments. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Feira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Feira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Feira region?
A: The Feira region offers a diverse network of over 70 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with approximately 15 easy, 22 moderate, and 34 difficult options to explore.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes around Santa Maria da Feira?
A: Yes, the region offers several routes suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' tags aren't always present, routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances are ideal. Look for the easier routes in the area, which often follow greenways or flatter sections, providing a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What are some popular circular touring cycling routes in Feira?
A: Many cyclists enjoy circular routes for their convenience. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1386845010/from-santa-maria-da-feira-to-ovar-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which connects the inland town with the coastal town of Ovar. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32187994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maceda Picnic Area 🍎 – Praia do Furadouro loop from Santa Maria da Feira&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural parkland and coastal scenery.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring bike trails in Feira?
A: Touring cycling routes around Feira offer a diverse landscape. You'll encounter a mix of urban areas, agricultural lands, and natural spaces, extending towards the coast. The area is characterized by its greenways, river valleys, and proximity to coastal towns like Ovar and Furadouro, providing varied natural and urban environments.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Feira?
A: The Feira region, like much of northern Portugal, generally experiences mild winters and warm, dry summers. Spring (April-June) and Autumn (September-October) are often considered the best seasons for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and less rainfall. Summer can be hot, especially inland, while winter rides are possible but may encounter more rain.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by scenic viewpoints or natural attractions?
A: Absolutely! The region has several points of interest. For instance, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/573237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrinha de Esmoriz Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful lake area. Routes near the coast, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32187994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maceda Picnic Area 🍎 – Praia do Furadouro loop from Santa Maria da Feira&lt;/a&gt;, offer coastal views and access to areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dune Forest of Ovar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with cafe stops or places to rest along the way?
A: Many routes in the Feira region pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops for refreshments. Coastal routes, in particular, often have amenities available. Planning your route with komoot allows you to identify potential stops and points of interest beforehand.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Feira?
A: The touring cycling routes in Feira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from greenways and river valleys to coastal stretches, and the mix of natural and urban environments that offer options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging touring cycling routes in the Feira area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Feira offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10408756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Santa Maria da Feira – Malaposta loop from São João de Ver&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 37.1-mile (59.7 km) trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32271550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Arrábida Bridge – Canide Norte Beach loop from Vila da Feira&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km.

Q: How do Feira's cycling trails compare to those in nearby Porto?
A: Feira's cycling trails offer a distinct experience compared to Porto. While Porto is known for its urban cycling infrastructure along the Douro River and coastal paths, Feira provides a more varied landscape that blends urban areas, agricultural lands, greenways, and river valleys, extending towards the coast. Feira's routes often feel more rural and offer a broader mix of terrain types.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites accessible by bike in the Feira region?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The iconic Castle of Santa Maria da Feira is a prominent landmark that features in routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10408756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Santa Maria da Feira – Malaposta loop from São João de Ver&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find historical churches such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6311238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igreja Matriz de Oliveira de Azeméis&lt;/a&gt;, often located in charming town centers like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2924364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovar Town Centre&lt;/a&gt;, which are great places to explore by bike.

Q: What should I wear for touring cycling in Feira?
A: Given the varied weather, especially outside of summer, it's best to dress in layers. Lightweight, breathable clothing is ideal for warmer months, while a waterproof and windproof jacket is recommended for cooler or wetter periods. Always wear a helmet, and consider padded shorts for comfort on longer rides. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also advisable year-round.
tips: The Feira Castle is a landmark of medieval military architecture, standing out for its defensive diversity. Its history dates back to prehistory and pagan worship, with emphasis on the Reconquista and the Crisis of 1383-1385. The castle houses a chapel and a baroque chaplaincy house and was classified as a National Monument in 1910.

Ovar, blessed by nature, presents varied scenarios with the estuary, sea and forest. Highlights include Blue Flag beaches, the biodiversity of the Buçaquinho Environmental Park and outdoor activities such as surfing and stand-up paddleboarding. The cultural richness is evident in the decorative tiles and religious heritage, with emphasis on the Churches of Válega and Cortegaça. The municipality is also known for its vibrant cultural and gastronomic life, including the famous Pão-de-Ló de Ovar.

waypoints.name: Castle of Santa Maria da Feira
